principle.name: Svelte 5 Runes
principle.description: Use Svelte 5 runes (`$state`, `$derived`, `$effect`, `$props`) instead of traditional Svelte stores or export let syntax.
principle.rationale: LegacyKit uses Svelte 5 for its reactive paradigm, which provides better performance and clearer state management.

principle.name: macOS Design Tokens
principle.description: Strictly use the CSS custom properties defined in the UI rebuild plan (e.g., `--color-accent`, `--radius-md`).
principle.rationale: Ensures a consistent, native-feeling GUI that adapts correctly to system light/dark mode preferences.

convention.rule: Use component-scoped CSS where possible, referencing global tokens.
Separate logical domains into specific folders (components, views, stores, api).

pitfall.why_problematic: Breaks dark mode support and ruins the native macOS feel.
UI Developer mode is restricted to the frontend. Modifying Rust code falls outside your permitted scope.
pitfall.correct_approach: Hand off to the Architect or Code mode for backend Tauri adjustments.
